entries.definition:
ḥ–m–ʾ stinking black mud, (of a well) to accumulate such mud,
to dredge; to be angry, in-laws. Of this root, two forms occur four
times in the Qurʾan: حَمَأً ḥamaʾ three times and حَمِئَة ḥamiʾatun
once.

حَمَأً ḥamaʾ [n.] stinking black mud (15:33) قَالَ لَمْ أَكُن لِّأَسْجُدَ لِبَشَرٍ
خَلَقْتَهُ مِن صَلْصَالٍ مِّنْ حَمَإٍ مَّسْنُونٍ and he answered, ‘It is not befitting for
me to bow to a mortal You created from clay formed of stinking
black mud.’

حَمِئَة ḥamiʾatun (also read as حَامِيَة ḥāmiyatun) [quasi-act. part.
fem.] foul, muddy, murky, stinking mud (18:86) حَتَّى إِذَا بَلَغَ مَغْرِبَ
الشَّمْسِ وَجَدَهَا تَغْرُبُ فِي عَيْنٍ حَمِئَةٍ until when he reached the
setting-place/time of the sun, he found it set into a murky pool.
